mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects accelerating real output, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO month-over-month growth is positive, over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling household income, which reduces consumption expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, the S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates demand overheating that will force future monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and PAYEMS does not significantly predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, with interaction term t-stat below 2 or directional accuracy under 55%, over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals reviving consumer demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During post-shock recovery periods, RSAFS month-over-month growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns, producing a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 0.25% signals an exceptionally accommodative monetary policy that suppresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding 3% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 3%, the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect strength in residential investment and construction activity, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month HOUST changes coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 0.4% erodes real corporate margins and triggers monetary policy tightening fears, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month-over-month, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
